mission.title: Install and Configure Milvus
mission.description: Milvus is a high-performance, cloud-native vector database designed for scalable vector approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search. It is ideal for AI applications that require efficient organization and retrieval of vast amounts of unstructured data. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management versus the performance benefits for large-scale vector searches.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Milvus CLI to interact with the Milvus database.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/releases/download/v2.5.0/milvus-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf milvus-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv milvus-cli /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Milvus CLI binary.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create milvus-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30.0

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials milvus-cluster --zone us-central1-a

```

> Fetch credentials for the cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials milvus-cluster --zone us-central1-a

steps.title: Verify Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes are up and running in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install Milvus
steps.description:
Deploy Milvus on the Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add milvus https://milvus-io.github.io/milvus-helm

helm install milvus milvus/milvus --version 2.5.0 --namespace milvus --create-namespace

```

> Add the Milvus Helm repository.
> Install Milvus in the 'milvus' namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for Milvus.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

```

> Deploy monitoring for Milvus.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description:
Be aware of potential costs associated with running a GKE cluster.

```bash

echo 'Remember to monitor your GKE usage to avoid unexpected charges.'

```

> Cost management reminder.
commands: echo 'Remember to monitor your GKE usage to avoid unexpected charges.'

uninstall.title: Uninstall Milvus
uninstall.description:
Remove Milvus from the Kubernetes cluster. This will delete all associated data.

```bash

helm uninstall milvus --namespace milvus

kubectl delete namespace milvus

```

> Uninstall the Milvus release.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Milvus
upgrade.description:
Backup existing data, upgrade Milvus, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl cp milvus-pod:/data /backup/milvus-data

helm upgrade milvus milvus/milvus --version 2.6.11 --namespace milvus

kubectl get pods --namespace milvus

```

> Backup existing data from Milvus.
> Upgrade Milvus to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Milvus'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Milvus service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Milvus pods with 'kubectl get pods --namespace milvus'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resource limits in your Helm values.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be a version mismatch or incorrect configuration.

**Fix:**
Check the Helm release status with 'helm status milvus --namespace milvus'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Milvus pod may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace milvus'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new vector indexing algorithms and improved query performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.5, ensure to update your indexing configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the Milvus service running with 'kubectl get pods --namespace milvus' showing all pods in 'Running' status.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters get-credentials milvus-cluster --zone us-central1-a

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
